stemHtml:
Refer to Alicante chart 11-2 from Jeppesen GSPRM 2017.
An aircraft is flying outbound on radial 298 from ALT VOR where there is a MAX speed for the base turn of the procedure. Why should this maximum speed be adhered to?

options.a: To avoid proximity with nearby terrain. If exceeded, the turn radius will increase and aircraft will fly close to terrain west of the airport.
options.b: There is a risk of overshooting the turn, ending up too high before final approach fix.
options.c: If the speed is adhered to, the aircraft will line up exactly with the ILS approach track.
options.d: The turn radius will decrease and aircraft will fly closer to the airport.
